source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 1. 2차 CE 인허가/Attachment 09. Usability Engineering File/09-03. Usability Validation Report (A-TCF-5-040).docx

document.title: Usability Validation Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector documenting user testing, task completion results, residual usability risk, and referenced usability/risk standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports usability engineering file evidence for ADD by documenting summative usability evaluation results and conclusions.
keyPoints: ADD is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Seven participants with medical device education and a doctor or medical radiology technician equivalent licence participated in the usability test.
Nine usability tasks were evaluated and each reported 100% completion with Pass result.
The report concludes residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable, with re-evaluation when new use-error issues are raised.

entities.name: ADD
entities.evidence: This device (ADD) intended use is as follows:

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: ADD(Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

entities.name: R&D team
entities.evidence: Prepared R&D team Jung, Woosuk APR/02/2021

entities.name: R&D Manager
entities.evidence: Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin APR/02/2021

entities.name: Quality Manager
entities.evidence: Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu APR/02/2021

concepts.name: Usability validation
concepts.evidence: The usability of ADD have been validated according to the usability verification & evaluation plan.

concepts.name: Summative evaluation
concepts.evidence: The summative evaluation has been performed according to:

concepts.name: Task completion success rate
concepts.evidence: Task Completion Success Rate - Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. - Result: 100 % (Pass)

concepts.name: Residual risk regarding usability
concepts.evidence: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

concepts.name: Use error re-evaluation
concepts.evidence: The re-evaluation of usability will be performed once new use-error issues are raised.

concepts.name: Not for mammography
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The document is a usability validation report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number is A-TCF-5-040 and the revision is Rev.1.
The document approval-related date is APR/02/2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Jung, Woosuk APR/02/2021 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin APR/02/2021 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu APR/02/2021

evidence.claim: Revision 1 was dated APR/02/2021 and revised the product name.

ADD is intended to obtain information necessary for patient diagnosis through anatomical structures obtained by film or image processing examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient through the anatomical structure of human body obtained by a film or an image processing (workstation) process of examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR (Portable flat panel).

evidence.claim: ADD is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Seven participants were involved in the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants were involved in a usability test to ensure stable results.

evidence.claim: Participants were people with medical device education and a licence equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.
evidence.evidenceText: Person who receive education about medical device and to have a licence equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.

evidence.claim: Each usability task had a criterion of 100% completion and the result was 100% Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Task Completion Success Rate - Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. - Result: 100 % (Pass)

evidence.claim: The evaluated usability tasks included user manual, power cord connection, cable connection, main power, PC software settings, battery handling, patient arrangement, indicators, and cleaning.
evidence.evidenceText: ACT #1) Usability of User manual ACT #2) Connecting the power cord ACT #3) Cable code connection ACT #4) Main power button action ACT #5) PC software settings ACT #6) Battery charging, change ACT #7) Arrange the patient ACT #8) Indicators ACT #9) Cleaning

evidence.claim: The usability of ADD was validated according to the usability verification and evaluation plan.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability of ADD have been validated according to the usability verification & evaluation plan.

evidence.claim: The report concludes usability residual risk is minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

Reference EN ISO 14971:2012 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices EN 60601-1-6:2010 IEC60601-1-6:2010/AMD1:2013 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability EN 62366:2008 IEC 62366:2007+AM1[2015] Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; authoritySignal is general.
Source metadata productSignal references HAD/A/F 1417-1717, while the source text identifies the product as ADD; only ADD is used as document product model.
Several acceptance criteria use wording such as 'over 100%' while the stated completion goal and reported results are 100%.
